Abraham believed God, and it was ... him for righteousness

name: Abraham believed God
notes: Genesis 15:6 records that Abraham believed the LORD and that it was counted to him for righteousness, the seed-text of justification by faith. The New Testament epistles quote this verse three times, each citing it as the ground of righteousness reckoned apart from works: Romans 4:3, Galatians 3:6, and James 2:23. All three share the near-identical wording 'Abraham believed God, and it was ... him for righteousness,' differing only in the verb they use for the reckoning. Romans says 'counted,' Galatians 'accounted,' and James 'imputed' — three complementary vantages on the one act of God crediting faith as righteousness. James further adds that the scripture 'was fulfilled' and that Abraham 'was called the Friend of God,' a detail unique to that account that reads in place.
